steps.title: Convert slope into a second point
steps.content: Use slope-intercept form $g(x)=mx+b$. The slope is $6$, so $m=6$. The point $(3,50)$ is on the graph.

steps.title: Fit the equation
steps.content: Substitute $(3,50)$ into $g(x)=6x+b$:$$50=6(3)+b$$ $$50=18+b\Rightarrow b=32$$So the equation is $g(x)=6x+32$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $g(x) = 32x$ has slope $32$, and the remaining alternative swaps the slope and intercept. $g(x) = 6a$ is not the right expression in terms of $x$. $g(x) = 6x + 32$, $g(x)=6x+32$, has slope $6$ and gives $g(3)=50$. $g(x) = 6x + 32$ is correct.
